flow.no: Set the thermostat to 'Cool' and wait for a few minutes.
flow.title: Is the thermostat set to 'Cool'?
flow.field_insight: Always check the thermostat first; it's a common oversight.
flow.interpretation: The thermostat was not set correctly, causing the AC to not cool.

flow.title: Is the AC unit receiving power?
actions: Check if the AC unit is running
flow.field_insight: Sometimes, the breaker can trip without obvious signs.
flow.interpretation: If the unit is not powered, it won't cool.

flow.no: Proceed to check for refrigerant issues.
flow.yes: Replace or clean the air filter.
flow.title: Is the air filter dirty?
actions: Inspect the air filter for dust and debris
flow.field_insight: Regularly changing the filter can improve efficiency.
flow.interpretation: A dirty filter restricts airflow, preventing cooling.

flow.no: Call a technician for further diagnosis.
flow.yes: Reset the breaker and check the AC again.
flow.title: Is the circuit breaker tripped?
flow.field_insight: If it trips again, there may be a deeper electrical problem.
flow.interpretation: A tripped breaker indicates an electrical issue.
